name: Muela Grande and Castillo de Xiquena Loop
name_original: Castillo de Xiquena – Vélez Rubio por el Collado de los Pintores y Solana de las Muelas
description: This moderate 20.6-mile mountain bike route in Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez offers historical castles and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vélez-Rubio offers diverse terrain within southeastern Spain's Comarca de Los Vélez. The region is characterized by mountains, forests, and streams, providing varied riding experiences. A significant portion of the trails are located within the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park, featuring native pine forests and rugged mountain ranges. Riders can expect routes with notable elevation gains and a mix of single tracks and technical sections. Best mountain bike trails around Vélez-Rubio The most popular mountain bike trail is Muela Grande and Castillo de Xiquena Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical sites with panoramic views within the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park Loop, a difficult 32.4 miles (52.1 km) path. This route winds through the protected natural park, featuring significant elevation changes and varied microclimates. Local mountain bikers also love the El Castellón Loop from Vélez-Rub, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. This difficult route includes substantial elevation gain. Mountain biking around Vélez-Rubio is defined by rugged mountains, extensive pine forests, and diverse terrain including single tracks and technical sections. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Vélez-Rubio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vélez-Rubio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The terrain around Vélez-Rubio is highly diverse, ranging from rugged mountains and extensive pine forests to varied sections including "flowy" single tracks and more technically demanding "super chunky" sections with rock gardens and chutes. You'll find significant elevation gains, with some routes reaching 3,000 feet or more, offering a challenging and exhilarating experience.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vélez-Rubio?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails available around Vélez-Rubio, catering to various skill levels. These include 2 easy routes, 27 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to advanced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Yes, there are options suitable for families. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217363691/velez-rubio-y-sus-alrededores-circular-en-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velez Rubio y sus alrededores — circular en BTT&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 10.2 miles (16.5 km) long and is classified as easy, making it a good choice for a less strenuous outing.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Advanced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217306700/sierra-de-maria-los-velez-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.4 miles (52.1 km) path with substantial elevation changes. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217320695/el-castellon-loop-from-velez-rub" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castellón Loop from Vélez-Rub&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.3 miles (31.0 km) long and also features significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Vélez-Rubio is an excellent destination for outdoor activities due to its favorable climate. While specific weather conditions can vary, the region generally offers good riding conditions for much of the year. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially during the hotter summer months or cooler winter periods.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Vélez-Rubio are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017516134/muela-grande-and-castillo-de-xiquena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muela Grande and Castillo de Xiquena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.6 miles (33.2 km) circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1217346955/desde-velez-rubio-al-castillo-de-nogalte-circular-en-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desde Vélez Rubio al Castillo de Nogalte — circular en BTT&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 54.8 miles (88.2 km) loop.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Many trails pass through or near notable attractions. You can encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2784897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caños de Caravaca Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which provides year-round crystal-clear mountain water. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views and diverse microclimates.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest near the trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the trails, the area is rich in history. You can visit the majestic 16th-century Renaissance &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Rubio&lt;/a&gt; itself is known for its traditional whitewashed houses and vibrant local culture. Religious sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Vélez-Rubio?
A: The mountain biking experience in Vélez-Rubio is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging elevation gains, and the opportunity to explore the beautiful landscapes within the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Vélez-Rubio?
A: While Vélez-Rubio is well-suited for outdoor activities, specific public transport options directly to trailheads can be limited. It's generally recommended to plan your journey with personal transport or check local bus schedules for connections to the town, from where many routes are accessible.

Q: Where can I find parking when mountain biking in Vélez-Rubio?
A: Parking is typically available within the town of Vélez-Rubio itself, and at various points near popular trailheads, especially those within the Sierra de María-Los Vélez Natural Park. Look for designated parking areas or consult local maps for convenient spots to leave your vehicle before starting your ride.
tips: The path that runs near Collado de los Pintores offers a beautiful view of the peaks of Muela Grande (1,552 m.) and Piedra del Mediodía (1,502 m.), in the Sierra de María-Los Vélez.

Remains of an ancient fortress of Islamic origin erected at the top of a small hill near the Corneros River. The castle was located in a large border space between Granada and Castilla in a permanent state of war.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It's suitable for most skill levels but requires good fitness. The terrain can include moderate gradients and obstacles, aligning with an S2 rating on the Single Trail Scale, meaning you might encounter small roots, stones, and steps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, typical for mountain biking in the Murcia region. You can expect a mix of singletrack paths, rural tracks, and sections with rocky elements, technical turns, roots, and potentially steep or loose surfaces. Given its moderate rating, it balances less technical paths with some more challenging sections.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: A major highlight is the ruined Castillo de Xiquena, an ancient Islamic fortress. From the castle, you can enjoy panoramic views of other historic castles like Tirieza, Puentes, Vélez Rubio, and Vélez Blanco. The route also offers a View of Muela Grande, providing spectacular scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region generally offers good riding conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant weather. Summers can be very hot, so early morning rides are recommended if visiting during that season. Winters are usually mild, but check local weather forecasts for rain or colder conditions.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, trails within the Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez generally allow dogs, provided they are kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not provided, but access points for trails in this region often have designated parking areas, particularly near villages like Vélez Rubio or historical sites like Castillo de Xiquena. It's advisable to check local maps or community forums for the most current parking options before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike trail or to enter the Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in the Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking within the Parque Natural Sierra de María-Los Vélez, it's important to stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment and wildlife. Respect flora and fauna, do not litter, and be mindful of other trail users. Always follow any posted signs regarding speed limits or restricted areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other trails. You will find portions of the "semnda del charche" trail along this route, offering potential connections for longer rides or alternative paths.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 33.2-kilometer mountain bike route is just over 3 hours. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos or breaks, and the specific trail conditions on the day of your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes near Vélez Rubio, a town where you can find various facilities including cafes, pubs, and restaurants to refuel after your ride. It's a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the trail, as amenities might not be directly on the route.
